mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS over the prior month signals easing discount rates and a lower hurdle for equity valuation, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because falling policy rates then read as supportive rather than as a response to stress.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 1-month FEDFUNDS decline while CPIAUCSL is flat or falling, or Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A jump in UNRATE above its recent level indicates deteriorating labor demand and rising recession risk, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are marked down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with a 0.3pp 3-month rise in UNRATE are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A broad increase in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue momentum, which supports earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 1-month RSAFS growth does not coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of observations, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 in any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO combined with stable employment growth signals improving output per worker and less cyclical margin pressure, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and non-accelerating PAYEMS do not deliver above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate remains below 55% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls sharply after a prior peak, it signals a housing demand downturn that typically precedes weaker cyclical spending and lower equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not negative on average after a >5% HOUST drop from its 3-month average, or if triggered observations show positive returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: High FEDFUNDS only becomes bearish for equities when CPIAUCSL is also elevated, because restrictive real rates then compress valuation multiples and slow demand. Under that joint condition, S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is above its 6-month trend
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate/high-inflation condition does not produce below-zero 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the conditional Rank IC is not materially negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and a fall in UNRATE indicates broad labor-market strengthening that supports household income and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fail to outperform baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign hit rate is below 55% over any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarterly acceleration in GDPC1 during a late-cycle expansion signals resilient real demand that can support earnings and extend the bull market, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth accelerates versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP acceleration does not correspond to positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional average return is not above the unconditional mean by at least one standard error.
